name: Spaghetti Bolognese
steps: In a large heavy-bottomed pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced onion, grated carrot, and celery. Cook until the vegetables are softened and the onion is translucent, about 8-10 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the ground beef, breaking it apart with a wooden spoon, and cook until thoroughly browned and no longer pink.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes to caramelize it, which adds depth to the sauce. Pour in the red wine to deglaze the pan, scraping up all the brown bits from the bottom.
Add the crushed tomatoes and dried oregano. Bring the sauce to a boil, then reduce the heat to low. Simmer partially covered for at least 30-40 minutes to let the flavors develop.
While the sauce is simmering, bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a boil. Add the spaghetti and cook until al dente according to the package instructions.
Before draining the pasta, reserve about half a cup of the starchy pasta water. Drain the spaghetti in a colander.
Toss the pasta with the Bolognese sauce, adding a little pasta water if needed to help the sauce coat the noodles. Serve hot with fresh basil and a generous amount of grated parmesan.

name: Chicken Alfredo
steps: Season the chicken breasts generously with salt and pepper on both sides. In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken until golden brown and cooked through, about 6-7 minutes per side.
Remove the chicken from the pan and let it rest on a cutting board for 5 minutes before slicing it into strips. This preserves the juices.
Wipe the skillet clean and melt the butter over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant but not browned.
Pour in the heavy cream and bring to a gentle simmer. Allow it to simmer for 3-5 minutes until it begins to reduce slightly.
Meanwhile, boil the fettuccine in a large pot of salted water until al dente. Reserve a small amount of pasta water before draining.
Gradually whisk the grated parmesan cheese into the cream sauce until it is completely melted and the sauce is smooth. Season with a pinch of nutmeg and salt to taste.

Add the cooked pasta and sliced chicken to the skillet. Toss vigorously until every strand is coated in the creamy sauce. If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of the reserved pasta water. Garnish with fresh parsley.

name: Margherita Pizza
steps: Place a pizza stone or an inverted heavy baking sheet in the oven and preheat to its highest setting (usually 250°C/500°F) for at least 45 minutes to ensure a crisp crust.
On a surface dusted with semolina or flour, stretch the dough ball by hand. Start from the center and move outward to create a 12-inch circle, leaving the rim slightly thicker for the 'cornicione'.
Spread the crushed San Marzano tomatoes thinly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border at the edge for the crust.
Distribute the torn fresh mozzarella evenly over the sauce. Drizzle with half of the olive oil and add a tiny pinch of sea salt.
Carefully slide the pizza onto the hot stone using a pizza peel or a piece of parchment paper.
Bake for 8-12 minutes. The pizza is ready when the crust is charred in spots and the cheese is bubbly and slightly browned.
Immediately after removing from the oven, top with the fresh basil leaves (this prevents them from burning) and the remaining drizzle of olive oil.

name: Lasagna
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (375°F). In a medium bowl, whisk the ricotta cheese, egg, and 1/4 cup of the parmesan until the mixture is light and creamy.
Spread about half a cup of the Bolognese meat sauce on the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish to prevent the pasta from sticking.

Place a single layer of lasagna sheets over the sauce. Spread 1/3 of the ricotta mixture over the sheets, followed by a layer of sautéed spinach, 1/3 of the mozzarella, and a generous cup of meat sauce.
Repeat the layering process two more times: pasta sheets, ricotta, mozzarella, and meat sauce.
For the final layer, place the remaining pasta sheets and cover them completely with the rest of the meat sauce, ensuring no dry edges of pasta are visible.
Top with the remaining mozzarella and parmesan. Cover the dish tightly with aluminum foil, ensuring the foil doesn't touch the cheese.
Bake for 25 minutes. Remove the foil and bake for another 20-25 minutes until the top is golden-brown and bubbling. Let it rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing to ensure neat layers.

name: Penne Arrabbiata
steps: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil and add plenty of salt. Cook the penne pasta until it is al dente (firm to the bite).
While the pasta cooks, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the sliced garlic and red chili flakes.
Sauté for about 2 minutes until the garlic is golden but not browned. Be careful not to burn the chili flakes as they can become bitter.
Add the hand-crushed tomatoes and their juices to the pan. Season with a pinch of salt and simmer for 10-12 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Reserve about a quarter cup of the pasta water, then drain the penne.

Add the drained pasta and the chopped parsley to the skillet with the sauce. Increase the heat to high and toss vigorously for 1 minute so the sauce penetrates the ridges of the penne. Add pasta water if the sauce is too thick.

name: Carbonara
steps: Set a large pot of water to boil. Use less salt than usual because both the cheese and the guanciale are very salty.

In a large skillet, cook the diced guanciale over medium heat. Let the fat render out slowly until the meat becomes crispy and golden (about 8-10 minutes). Turn off the heat but leave the pan on the stove.
In a small stainless steel bowl, whisk together the whole eggs, the extra yolks, and the finely grated Pecorino Romano until a thick, uniform paste forms. Stir in half of the cracked black pepper.
Cook the spaghetti until al dente. Reserve a full cup of the starchy pasta water.
Using tongs, transfer the hot pasta directly from the pot into the skillet with the guanciale and its rendered fat. Toss well to coat.

VERY IMPORTANT: Ensure the skillet is not too hot (remove from the burner). Pour the egg and cheese mixture over the pasta while tossing it constantly with tongs. The residual heat from the pasta will cook the eggs into a creamy sauce without scrambling them.
Gradually add small splashes of the reserved pasta water while tossing until the sauce is silky, glossy, and coats every strand. Finish with the remaining black pepper.

name: Caprese Salad
steps: Wash and dry the tomatoes. Using a sharp serrated knife, slice them into rounds approximately 1cm thick.
Drain the mozzarella and slice it into rounds of similar thickness to the tomatoes.
On a large, flat serving platter, arrange the slices in an alternating sequence: tomato, then mozzarella, then a large basil leaf.
Drizzle the entire salad generously with the best quality extra virgin olive oil you have available.
If you prefer a sweeter touch, drizzle a small amount of aged balsamic vinegar or balsamic glaze over the slices.
Just before serving, sprinkle with flaky sea salt and freshly cracked black pepper. This prevents the salt from drawing out too much moisture from the tomatoes before it reaches the table.

name: Risotto
steps: In a wide saucepan, melt 1 tablespoon of butter and sauté the sliced mushrooms until they are deeply browned and their moisture has evaporated. Season with salt and set aside.
In the same pan, melt 2 more tablespoons of butter. Add the minced shallots and cook over medium heat until translucent and soft, about 3 minutes.
Add the rice to the pan and 'toast' it for 2 minutes, stirring constantly until the edges of the grains become translucent and it smells slightly nutty.
Pour in the white wine to deglaze the pan. Stir continuously until the wine has been completely absorbed by the rice.

Begin adding the hot broth one ladle at a time. Stir frequently; the friction helps release the starch from the rice, creating the creamy texture. Wait until the liquid is almost fully absorbed before adding the next ladle.
Continue this process for 18-20 minutes until the rice is tender but still has a slight bite (al dente).
Remove the pan from the heat. Stir in the cooked mushrooms, the remaining butter, and the grated parmesan. This step is called 'Mantecatura'. Cover and let it sit for 2 minutes before serving.

name: Bruschetta
steps: In a medium bowl, combine the diced tomatoes, minced garlic, basil, 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and pepper. Let the mixture sit at room temperature for at least 20 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and the tomatoes to release their juices.
Preheat a grill pan or your oven broiler.
Brush both sides of the bread slices with the remaining olive oil. Toast them until they are golden brown and very crisp.
While the bread is still hot, take a halved raw garlic clove and rub the cut side against the surface of each slice. The heat and texture of the bread will melt the garlic into the crust.
Stir the tomato mixture once more and spoon it generously onto the toasted bread slices. Make sure to include some of the flavorful marinating liquid.
Finish with a light drizzle of balsamic glaze and serve immediately while the bread is still warm.

name: Chicken Tacos
steps: Trim any excess fat from the chicken thighs and dice them into very small, uniform cubes (about 1cm). Toss the chicken in a bowl with the oregano, paprika, cumin, and salt.
Heat a large cast-iron skillet over high heat until it starts to smoke slightly. Add the oil and then the chicken in a single layer.
Let the chicken sear without moving it for 3-4 minutes to develop a deep brown crust. Stir and continue cooking for another 4-5 minutes until fully cooked through.

To warm the tortillas, place them directly over a gas flame for 10 seconds per side until they char slightly, or heat them in a dry pan until soft and pliable. Keep them warm wrapped in a clean kitchen towel.
Assemble the tacos by stacking two tortillas per taco. Add a generous portion of the charred chicken.
Top with the raw diced white onion and fresh cilantro. Serve with several lime wedges to be squeezed over the meat just before eating.

name: Beef Burrito
steps: In a medium skillet over medium-high heat, brown the ground beef until no longer pink. Drain any excess fat from the pan.
Add the taco seasoning and 1/4 cup of water. Simmer for 5 minutes until the liquid has reduced and thickened into a light sauce.
In a separate small pan or microwave, lightly warm the tortillas for 15 seconds to make them pliable and prevent tearing.
Lay the tortillas flat. In the center of each, layer the warm rice, black beans, seasoned beef, and shredded cheese.
Add a dollop of sour cream and a spoonful of salsa on top of the filling.
Fold the left and right sides of the tortilla inward over the filling, then roll from the bottom up as tightly as possible.
Optional: Place the rolled burritos seam-side down in a dry skillet over medium heat for 1 minute per side to create a crispy, golden crust.

name: Quesadilla
steps: Lightly butter one side of each tortilla using room-temperature butter.
Place one tortilla, buttered side down, into a large non-stick skillet over medium-low heat.
Spread the shredded cheese evenly over the entire surface of the tortilla in the pan. Top with jalapeño slices and cilantro.
Place the second tortilla on top, buttered side facing up.
Cook for about 3-4 minutes until the bottom tortilla is crisp and golden brown.
Carefully flip the quesadilla using a wide spatula. Cook for another 3 minutes until the cheese is completely melted and the second side is golden.
Slide onto a cutting board, let it rest for 1 minute so the cheese sets, then cut into 4 or 6 wedges with a pizza cutter.

name: Guacamole
steps: Slice the avocados in half, remove the pits, and scoop the flesh into a medium-sized mixing bowl.
Using a fork or a potato masher, mash the avocado until it reaches your desired consistency (traditional guac should have some small chunks remaining).
Immediately stir in the lime juice to provide flavor and prevent the avocado from browning.
Add the minced red onion, chopped cilantro, finely diced chili pepper, and salt.
Gently fold the ingredients together until well combined. Avoid over-mixing.
Taste and adjust salt or lime if necessary. Place plastic wrap directly on the surface of the guacamole if not serving immediately to prevent oxidation.
Serve with warm corn tortilla chips.

name: Enchiladas
steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Lightly grease a 9x13 inch baking dish.
In a bowl, toss the shredded chicken with 1/2 cup of the enchilada sauce and the diced onions to keep the filling moist.
To prevent the corn tortillas from cracking, wrap them in damp paper towels and microwave for 30 seconds, or quickly dip each one in warm sauce.
Place about 2-3 tablespoons of the chicken mixture in the center of a tortilla. Roll it tightly and place it seam-side down in the baking dish.
Repeat for all tortillas until the dish is full. Pour the remaining enchilada sauce evenly over the top.
Sprinkle the shredded cheese generously over the sauce. Cover the dish with aluminum foil.
Bake for 15 minutes, then remove the foil and bake for another 10 minutes until the cheese is bubbly and slightly browned. Garnish with cilantro.

name: Nachos
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (400°F). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Spread the tortilla chips in an even layer across the baking sheet, slightly overlapping them.
Distribute the black beans and half of the cheese sauce (or shredded cheese) over the chips.
Bake for 5 to 8 minutes until the chips are toasted and the cheese is fully melted and hot.
Remove from the oven and immediately top with diced tomatoes and jalapeño slices.
Add large dollops of guacamole and drizzle with the remaining cheese sauce and sour cream.
Serve immediately while hot to prevent the chips from becoming soggy.

name: Street Corn (Elote)
steps: Peel back the husks and remove the silk from the corn. Brush lightly with oil or butter.
Grill the corn over medium-high heat (on a BBQ or grill pan), turning occasionally, for about 10-12 minutes until charred in spots all over.
While the corn is still hot, brush each ear generously with a layer of mayonnaise.
Roll the corn in the crumbled Cotija cheese until it is well-coated on all sides.
Dust with chili powder or Tajín seasoning and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Serve immediately with lime wedges for squeezing over the corn.

name: Rice and Beans
steps: In a medium saucepan, heat a tablespoon of oil over medium heat. Add the onion and sauté until translucent (about 3 minutes).
Add the dry rice to the pan and stir for 1-2 minutes until the grains are slightly toasted and fragrant.
Pour in the vegetable broth and add the cumin, garlic powder, and a pinch of salt. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil.
Reduce the heat to the lowest setting, cover the pot with a tight-fitting lid, and simmer for 15-17 minutes without opening the lid.
Rinse and drain the black beans. Once the rice is cooked and the water is absorbed, gently fold the beans into the rice.
Cover again and let it sit off the heat for 5 minutes to allow the beans to warm through.
Fluff with a fork and garnish with fresh cilantro or lime juice if desired.

name: Chicken Fried Rice
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add the diced chicken and cook until fully browned and cooked through. Remove and set aside.
Pour the beaten eggs into the hot wok. Scramble them quickly until set but still soft, then remove and set aside with the chicken.
Add another tablespoon of oil and stir-fry the peas and carrots for 2 minutes until tender.
Add the cold rice to the wok. Use a spatula to break up any large clumps. Fry the rice for 3-5 minutes, stirring frequently, until it starts to toast.
Return the chicken and eggs to the pan. Pour the soy sauce and sesame oil over the mixture.
Toss everything together vigorously for 2 minutes to ensure the sauce is evenly distributed and the rice is steaming hot.
Top with sliced green onions and serve immediately.

name: Beef and Broccoli
steps: In a bowl, whisk together the sliced beef, soy sauce, and cornstarch. Let it marinate for at least 10 minutes to tenderize the meat.
Bring a small pot of water to a boil and blanch the broccoli florets for 2 minutes. Drain and immediately rinse with cold water to stop the cooking and keep them bright green.
Heat oil in a wok over very high heat. Add the beef in batches to avoid crowding the pan, searing quickly for 1-2 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Add the ginger and garlic to the wok and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the beef and broccoli to the wok. Pour in the oyster sauce and a splash of water if needed.
Stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until the sauce has thickened and coated everything evenly. Serve hot with steamed rice.

name: Sweet and Sour Chicken
steps: Pat the chicken cubes dry. Dip each piece into the beaten egg, then dredge in the flour until fully coated.
Heat about 1 inch of oil in a large heavy pot or deep fryer to 175°C (350°F).
Fry the chicken in batches for 4-5 minutes until golden brown and crispy. Drain on a wire rack or paper towels.
In a separate large skillet or wok, sauté the bell peppers and pineapple chunks over high heat for 3 minutes.
Lower the heat slightly and pour in the sweet and sour sauce. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the crispy chicken to the sauce and toss quickly to coat every piece while maintaining the crunch.
Serve immediately over a bed of white rice.

name: Lo Mein
steps: If using dried egg noodles, cook them in boiling water for 1-2 minutes less than the package instructions (parboiled). Rinse under cold water and drain well to prevent sticking.
In a small bowl, whisk together the light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil to create the seasoning base.
Heat a wok over high heat with 1 tablespoon of neutral oil. Once smoking slightly, add the garlic and stir-fry for 10 seconds.
Add the carrots and snow peas, tossing vigorously for 2 minutes. Add the shredded cabbage and continue to stir-fry until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Add the cooked noodles to the wok. Pour the sauce mixture over the noodles.
Using tongs or a wok spatula, toss everything over high heat for 3-4 minutes until the noodles are evenly coated in the sauce and everything is steaming hot.
Garnish with a drizzle of sesame oil and serve immediately.

name: Dumplings
steps: Place the finely chopped cabbage in a bowl with a pinch of salt. Let it sit for 10 minutes, then squeeze out as much water as possible using your hands or a cheesecloth.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground pork, drained cabbage, chives, ginger, soy sauce, rice wine, and sesame oil. Mix vigorously in one direction until the mixture becomes sticky.
Place one dumpling wrapper on your palm. Add a rounded teaspoon of filling to the center. Moisten the edges of the wrapper with a bit of water.
Fold the wrapper over the filling and pleat the edges to seal, ensuring there are no air bubbles trapped inside.
Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium heat with 1 tablespoon of oil. Arrange dumplings in the pan and fry for 2-3 minutes until the bottoms are golden brown.
Carefully pour 1/4 cup of water into the pan and immediately cover with a tight-fitting lid. Steam for 6-8 minutes until the water has evaporated and the wrappers are translucent.
Remove the lid and fry for another minute to re-crisp the bottoms. Serve with a soy-vinegar dipping sauce.

name: Kung Pao Chicken
steps: Toss the chicken cubes with soy sauce and cornstarch. Let marinate for 15 minutes to ensure the meat stays tender during high-heat frying.
Whisk the sauce: Combine hoisin sauce, a splash of soy sauce, and a teaspoon of sugar in a small bowl.
Heat a wok over high heat with oil. Add the dried chilies and Sichuan peppercorns. Stir-fry for 30 seconds until the chilies darken and the oil becomes fragrant.
Add the chicken cubes to the wok. Spread them out and sear for 2-3 minutes until browned on the outside.
Add the diced zucchini and continue to stir-fry for 2 more minutes.
Pour the sauce mixture into the wok. Toss rapidly so the sauce thickens and glazes the chicken and vegetables evenly.
Stir in the roasted peanuts at the very last second to maintain their crunch. Serve immediately with white rice.

name: Egg Rolls
steps: In a large skillet, cook the ground pork until browned. Add the cabbage, carrots, ginger, and garlic. Sauté for 3-4 minutes until the vegetables are limp and the moisture has evaporated. Let the mixture cool completely.
Lay one egg roll wrapper on a clean surface in a diamond shape. Place 2 tablespoons of the filling in the lower center.
Fold the bottom corner up over the filling, then fold in the left and right side corners.
Roll upward tightly. Brush the top corner with a little cornstarch slurry (cornstarch mixed with water) to seal the roll.
Heat oil in a heavy pot to 175°C (350°F).
Deep fry the egg rolls in batches for 3-5 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are deep golden brown and crispy.
Drain on a wire rack or paper towels. Serve with sweet chili sauce or hot mustard.

name: Hot and Sour Soup
steps: In a large pot, bring the chicken broth to a gentle boil.
Add the tofu matchsticks and bamboo shoots. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the soy sauce and white pepper. The white pepper provides the unique 'heat' of this soup, so adjust to your preference.
Slowly stir in the rice vinegar. Taste the broth; it should be balanced between sour and savory.
Whisk the cornstarch slurry once more and slowly pour it into the boiling soup while stirring. Let the soup thicken for 2 minutes.
Turn off the heat or reduce to a very low simmer. Slowly drizzle the beaten egg into the soup in a circular motion. Let it sit for 10 seconds before gently stirring to create delicate 'egg ribbons'.
Finish with a drizzle of sesame oil and garnish with chopped green onions.

name: Sushi Rolls
steps: Prepare the sushi rice by gently folding rice vinegar, sugar, and salt into warm, freshly cooked short-grain rice. Let it cool to room temperature (do not refrigerate).
Place a nori sheet, shiny side down, on a bamboo rolling mat covered with plastic wrap.
Wet your hands with vinegared water to prevent sticking. Spread a thin, even layer of rice over the nori, leaving 1 inch of bare nori at the top.
Lay strips of salmon and cucumber across the center of the rice. Apply a very thin line of wasabi if desired.
Using the mat, lift the edge closest to you and roll tightly over the filling. Press firmly to ensure a compact roll.
Moisten the bare edge of the nori with a drop of water to seal the roll completely.
Using a very sharp, wet knife, slice the roll in half, then into quarters, then into eighths. Wipe and re-wet the knife between every cut for clean edges.

name: Chicken Teriyaki
steps: In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar, and grated ginger to create the teriyaki sauce.
Place chicken thighs skin-side down in a cold skillet. Turn the heat to medium and cook slowly for 8-10 minutes. This renders the fat and makes the skin extra crispy.
Once the skin is deep golden and crispy, flip the chicken and cook for another 3-4 minutes.
Pour off any excess fat from the pan. Add the teriyaki sauce to the skillet.
Bring the sauce to a boil. Continuously spoon the thickening sauce over the chicken (glazing) until the sauce is glossy and reduced by half.
Remove the chicken from the pan and let it rest for 2 minutes before slicing into strips.
Serve over steamed white rice with the remaining sauce drizzled over, garnished with sesame seeds and steamed broccoli.

name: Ramen
steps: In a medium pot, bring the stock to a simmer. In a small bowl, mix the miso paste with a few tablespoons of hot stock until smooth, then stir it back into the pot. Do not boil the broth after adding miso to preserve its flavor.
Boil a separate pot of water and cook the ramen noodles according to package instructions. Drain well.
If using pork belly, sear it in a hot pan until crispy on the edges.
Prepare your serving bowl: Place the cooked noodles in the bottom and pour the hot miso broth over them.
Arrange the toppings neatly: Add the meat slices, corn, bamboo shoots, and the halved soft-boiled egg.
Place a square of nori on the side of the bowl so it stays crispy.
Garnish with chopped green onions and a drizzle of chili oil (optional) before serving immediately.

name: Tempura
steps: Make small incisions along the belly of the shrimp to prevent them from curling when fried. Press them flat on the cutting board.
In a bowl, lightly whisk the ice-cold water and egg yolk. Add the tempura flour and stir briefly with chopsticks. Do not overmix; lumps are essential for a crispy texture.
Heat oil in a deep pot to 180°C (350°F).
Dust the shrimp and vegetable slices lightly with dry flour, then dip into the cold batter.
Carefully drop into the hot oil. Fry the vegetables for 2-3 minutes and the shrimp for 2 minutes until very light golden and airy.
Drain on a wire rack to allow steam to escape so the coating remains crunchy.
Serve immediately with tempura dipping sauce (tentsuyu) mixed with grated daikon radish.

name: Miso Soup
steps: In a small pot, bring the dashi stock to a gentle simmer over medium heat.
Add the dried wakame seaweed and silken tofu cubes. Simmer for 2 minutes until the seaweed has fully expanded.

Place the miso paste into a small strainer. Submerge the bottom of the strainer into the stock and use a spoon to dissolve the paste directly into the broth. This ensures no lumps are left in the soup.
Immediately turn off the heat once the miso is incorporated. Boiling miso destroys its delicate probiotic benefits and aromatic flavor.
Ladle into small bowls and garnish with finely sliced green onions.
Serve warm as a side dish or starter.

name: Yakisoba
steps: Prepare the yakisoba noodles by placing them in a colander and rinsing with warm water to loosen the strands. Drain well.
In a large skillet or flat griddle (teppan), heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium-high heat. Add the pork belly slices and cook until browned and crispy.
Add the onions and carrots to the pan. Sauté for 2 minutes until they begin to soften.
Add the cabbage and continue stir-frying for another 2-3 minutes until the cabbage is wilted but still has a slight crunch.
Place the noodles on top of the meat and vegetables. Add 2 tablespoons of water or sake to create steam, then cover the pan for 1 minute to heat the noodles through.
Remove the lid and pour the yakisoba sauce over the noodles. Use tongs to toss everything together vigorously for 2-3 minutes until the sauce is evenly caramelized.
Plate the noodles and garnish generously with powdered seaweed (aonori) and a pile of pickled red ginger on the side.

name: Katsu Chicken
steps: Place the chicken breasts between two sheets of plastic wrap. Use a meat mallet or a rolling pin to pound them to a uniform thickness of about 1.5 cm. This ensures even cooking.
Season the chicken on both sides with salt and pepper.
Set up a breading station with three shallow bowls: one with flour, one with the beaten egg, and one with panko breadcrumbs.
Coat each chicken breast in flour, shaking off the excess. Dip into the egg wash, then press firmly into the panko until completely covered.
Heat about 1 cm of oil in a large frying pan over medium heat (approx. 170°C).
Fry the chicken for 4-5 minutes per side until the crust is deep golden brown and the internal temperature reaches 75°C.
Let the chicken rest on a wire rack for 2 minutes to keep the bottom from getting soggy. Slice into 2 cm strips and serve immediately with tonkatsu sauce and a side of cold, shredded cabbage.

name: Pad Thai
steps: Soak the rice noodles in room-temperature water for 30-40 minutes until pliable but still slightly firm. Do not boil them.
Prepare the sauce: In a small bowl, whisk together the tamarind paste, fish sauce, and palm sugar until the sugar is dissolved.
Heat a wok over high heat with 2 tablespoons of oil. Add the shrimp and tofu cubes. Sear until the shrimp turn pink and the tofu is golden. Remove only the shrimp to prevent overcooking.
Add the drained noodles to the wok. Pour the sauce mixture over the noodles and stir-fry vigorously. The noodles will absorb the sauce and soften.
Push the noodles to one side of the wok. Crack the egg into the empty space, scramble it quickly, and then toss it with the noodles.
Add the bean sprouts and half of the crushed peanuts. Return the shrimp to the wok.
Toss everything together for 1 minute until well combined and the sprouts have just started to wilt. Serve with lime wedges, extra peanuts, and chili flakes on the side.

name: Green Curry
steps: Open the can of coconut milk without shaking it. Spoon out 3 tablespoons of the thick cream from the top and put it into a medium pot.
Heat the cream over medium heat until it starts to bubble and release oil. Add the green curry paste and stir-fry for 2 minutes until very fragrant.
Add the sliced chicken to the pot. Stir to coat the meat in the paste and cook until the outside of the chicken is opaque.
Pour in the remaining coconut milk and add the bamboo shoots and eggplants.
Bring to a gentle simmer (do not boil rapidly) and cook for 10 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Season with fish sauce and palm sugar. Adjust to balance the salty and sweet notes.
Turn off the heat. Stir in the fresh Thai basil leaves—the residual heat will wilt them and release their aroma. Serve with fragrant jasmine rice.

name: Red Curry
steps: Similar to the green curry method, heat a few tablespoons of coconut cream in a pot until the oil separates.
Stir-fry the red curry paste in the cream for 2-3 minutes until the aromas are released and the oil turns red.
Add the sliced beef and sear briefly until browned on the edges.
Pour in the rest of the coconut milk and add the bamboo shoots and red bell peppers.
Simmer gently for 12-15 minutes until the beef is tender and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Season with fish sauce and palm sugar to taste.
Finish by stirring in the fresh Thai basil leaves. Serve hot with jasmine rice or rice noodles.

name: Thai Basil Chicken
steps: Using a mortar and pestle, pound the chilies and garlic into a coarse, wet paste.
Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat with 2 tablespoons of oil. Add the garlic-chili paste and stir-fry for 30 seconds until the aroma fills the room (be careful of the fumes!).
Add the ground chicken to the wok. Use your spatula to break up the meat and stir-fry until it is fully browned and almost dry.
Stir in the oyster sauce, dark soy sauce, fish sauce, and sugar. Toss well to coat the meat evenly.
Add the basil leaves to the wok and immediately remove from heat. Stir for 15 seconds until the leaves are wilted.
Serve the chicken over steaming white rice. Traditionally, this is topped with a 'Thai-style' fried egg (fried in lots of oil so the edges are crispy and the yolk is runny).

name: Mango Sticky Rice
steps: Soak the sticky rice in water for at least 4 hours (overnight is best). Drain and rinse.
Steam the rice in a bamboo steamer or a steamer basket for 25-30 minutes until the grains are tender and translucent.
While the rice is steaming, combine the coconut cream, sugar, and salt in a small saucepan. Heat over low heat until the sugar is dissolved (do not boil).
Transfer the hot, freshly steamed rice into a bowl. Pour 3/4 of the warm coconut mixture over the rice. Cover and let it sit for 20 minutes to allow the rice to absorb the cream.
To serve, place a portion of the coconut rice on a plate. Arrange fresh mango slices alongside the rice.
Drizzle the remaining coconut cream over the rice and sprinkle with toasted mung beans for a traditional crunch.

name: Tom Yum Soup
steps: Peel the shrimp but leave the tails on. If using head-on shrimp, keep the heads to flavor the broth.
Bring 3 cups of water (or light chicken stock) to a boil in a pot. Add the lemongrass, galangal, and lime leaves. Simmer for 5 minutes until the water is aromatic.
Add the mushrooms and the Thai chili paste. Stir until the paste is dissolved.
Add the shrimp to the pot. Cook for 2-3 minutes until they turn pink and are just cooked through.
Turn off the heat. This is important to preserve the flavor of the lime juice.
Stir in the fish sauce and lime juice. Taste the broth; it should be a sharp balance of spicy, sour, and salty.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and sliced chilies if desired. Serve hot.

name: Butter Chicken
steps: Marinate the chicken pieces in yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, and a pinch of salt for at least 30 minutes.
Pan-fry the chicken in a skillet over high heat until charred and cooked through. Set aside.
In a separate pot, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the tomato puree and simmer for 10 minutes until it thickens and the oil starts to separate.
Add the garam masala and the cooked chicken to the tomato sauce. Stir well.
Slowly pour in the heavy cream while stirring constantly. The sauce should turn a beautiful orange-pink color.
Crush the dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) between your palms and sprinkle them into the sauce. This provides the signature 'restaurant' aroma.
Simmer for another 5 minutes on very low heat. Garnish with a swirl of cream and serve with garlic naan.

name: Chicken Tikka Masala
steps: Whisk together the yogurt, turmeric, cumin, and coriander. Toss with the chicken cubes and marinate for at least 2 hours (or overnight).
Thread the chicken onto skewers and grill at high heat until charred and cooked through (or bake at 220°C for 15-20 mins).
In a large saucepan, sauté minced garlic and ginger in oil. Add the tomato sauce and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the heavy cream and bring to a very gentle simmer.
Add the grilled chicken pieces (the 'Tikka') to the creamy sauce (the 'Masala').
Simmer for 5-10 minutes to allow the flavors to combine. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve with basmati rice.

name: Biryani
steps: Marinate the chicken in yogurt, biryani spices, ginger-garlic paste, and salt for at least 1 hour.
Soak the basmati rice for 30 minutes, then parboil in a large pot of water with whole spices (cloves, cardamom) until 70% cooked. Drain and set aside.
In a heavy-bottomed pot (Handi), heat ghee and sear the marinated chicken until browned on the outside but not fully cooked.
Layering: Spread half of the parboiled rice over the chicken. Sprinkle half of the fried onions, mint, and coriander. Add the remaining rice as the final layer.
Dissolve saffron in warm milk and drizzle it over the top layer of rice to create the signature yellow patches.
Seal the pot with a tight-fitting lid or aluminum foil to trap the steam (this is the 'Dum' method).
Cook on very low heat for 20-25 minutes. Let it rest for 10 minutes before gently fluffing and serving with raita.

name: Naan
steps: In a large bowl, combine warm water, sugar, and yeast. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until frothy.
Add the flour, yogurt, and a pinch of salt. Knead the dough for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Cover and let it rise in a warm place for 2 hours.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 6-8 equal balls.
Roll each ball into an oval shape about 0.5 cm thick using a rolling pin.
Heat a cast-iron skillet or pizza stone over very high heat. Place the naan in the dry pan.
Wait for bubbles to form on the surface (about 1 minute), then flip and cook the other side for 30-60 seconds until charred spots appear.
Immediately brush the hot naan with melted butter mixed with minced garlic and sprinkle with nigella seeds.

name: Chana Masala
steps: In a medium pot, heat oil over medium heat. Sauté the minced onion until deep golden brown (about 10 minutes).
Add the ginger-garlic paste and slit green chilies. Cook for 2 minutes until the raw smell disappears.
Stir in the tomato puree and the chana masala spices. Cook until the oil begins to separate from the sauce.
Add the rinsed chickpeas and half a cup of water. Use a spoon to lightly mash a few chickpeas to thicken the gravy.
Simmer for 15 minutes on low heat, allowing the chickpeas to absorb the spices.
Stir in the amchur powder for a traditional tangy finish.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve with bhatura or basmati rice.

name: Dal
steps: Rinse the lentils thoroughly. In a pot, combine lentils, 3 cups of water, turmeric, and salt. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 20-25 minutes until the lentils are completely soft and mushy.
While the lentils are cooking, prepare the 'Tarka' (tempering).
In a small pan, heat the ghee over medium-high heat. Add the cumin seeds and hing. Wait for the seeds to sizzle and pop.
Add the sliced garlic and dried red chilies to the ghee. Fry until the garlic is golden brown and fragrant.
Pour the hot ghee mixture directly over the cooked lentils. It will sizzle—immediately cover the pot for 2 minutes to trap the smoky aroma.
Stir gently and serve hot with steamed rice or chapati.

name: Samosas
steps: In a skillet, sauté cumin seeds and minced green chilies in a little oil. Add the boiled potato cubes and peas.
Sprinkle the garam masala and salt. Mash the potatoes slightly with a spatula as you sauté them for 5 minutes. Let the filling cool completely.
Fold your pastry into a cone shape. Place a spoonful of the potato filling inside.
Seal the edges of the cone with a little water, pressing firmly to ensure no oil gets inside while frying.
Heat oil in a deep fryer or wok to 170°C.
Fry the samosas in small batches for 6-8 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are golden brown and very crispy.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot with mint-tamarind chutney.

name: Bibimbap
steps: Sauté each vegetable (carrots, mushrooms) separately in a pan with a drop of sesame oil and a pinch of salt. This keeps their individual flavors distinct.
Blanch the spinach and bean sprouts in boiling water for 30 seconds, then shock in ice water and squeeze dry. Season with a little sesame oil.
Prepare the serving bowl by placing a large portion of warm rice at the bottom.
Arrange the colorful vegetables in neat piles around the perimeter of the bowl on top of the rice.
Place the sunny-side-up fried egg in the center of the bowl.
Add a generous dollop of Gochujang sauce and a final drizzle of sesame oil.
Serve immediately. The traditional way to eat it is to mix everything thoroughly until the rice and vegetables are fully coated in the red sauce.

name: Korean BBQ Beef (Bulgogi)
steps: In a bowl, mix the soy sauce, sugar, sesame oil, garlic, and grated pear to create the marinade.
Add the thinly sliced beef to the marinade. Ensure every slice is coated. Let it marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes (up to 4 hours).
Heat a large skillet or cast-iron grill pan over very high heat until it starts to smoke.
Add the beef in a single layer. Do not crowd the pan; cook in batches if necessary.
Sear the beef for 1-2 minutes per side until caramelized and slightly charred. The thin slices cook very quickly.
Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds. Serve with lettuce leaves (Ssam) and ssamjang sauce for wrapping.
equipment: Cast Iron Skillet or Grill Pan

name: Kimchi Fried Rice
steps: Heat a tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chopped kimchi and sauté for 3 minutes until it softens and smells fragrant.
Add the Gochujang paste and kimchi juice to the pan. Stir until combined.
Add the cold rice to the skillet. Use a spatula to break up any large clumps and mix thoroughly with the kimchi base.
Stir-fry for 4-5 minutes, pressing the rice down occasionally to get some crispy bits at the bottom.
Turn off the heat and stir in the sesame oil.
Top with a sunny-side-up fried egg and strips of roasted seaweed. Serve hot.

name: Tteokbokki
steps: If the rice cakes are frozen, soak them in room-temperature water for 10 minutes to soften.
In a shallow pan, bring the anchovy broth to a boil.
Whisk in the Gochujang, Gochugaru, and sugar until completely dissolved.
Add the rice cakes and fish cakes to the boiling liquid.
Reduce heat to medium and simmer for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent the rice cakes from sticking to the pan.
Continue cooking until the sauce has thickened into a rich, red glaze that coats the chewy rice cakes.
Garnish with sliced green onions and add halved boiled eggs to the sauce before serving.

name: Korean Fried Chicken
steps: Pat the chicken wings completely dry with paper towels. Toss them in a bowl with cornstarch until every wing is thinly and evenly coated.
Heat oil in a deep fryer or heavy pot to 170°C.
Double Frying Step 1: Fry the wings for 7-8 minutes. They should be pale golden and cooked through. Drain and let them rest for 5 minutes.
Double Frying Step 2: Increase the oil heat to 190°C. Fry the wings again for 2-3 minutes until they are a deep golden brown and extremely crispy. This is the secret to Korean fried chicken.
In a small saucepan, heat the Gochujang, ketchup, honey, and garlic over low heat until it forms a sticky glaze.
In a large bowl, toss the hot, double-fried wings with the glaze until thoroughly coated.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve immediately with pickled radish.

name: Omelette
steps: In a medium bowl, whisk the eggs vigorously with salt and white pepper until no streaks of white remain and the mixture is slightly frothy.
Place an 8-inch non-stick skillet over medium-low heat. Add the butter and swirl it around until melted and foamy, but do not let it brown.
Pour in the egg mixture. Using a heat-resistant rubber spatula, stir the eggs quickly in a circular motion while shaking the pan back and forth.
Once the eggs have formed small curds but the top still looks slightly wet (the 'baveuse' state), stop stirring and use the spatula to smooth the eggs into an even layer.
Tuck the far edge of the omelette over itself, then roll it toward the handle of the pan.
Invert the omelette onto a warm plate so the seam is on the bottom. The exterior should be smooth, pale yellow, and free of any brown spots.
Brush the top with a tiny bit of extra butter for shine and garnish with minced chives.

name: Ratatouille
steps: Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven. Sauté the eggplant cubes until golden and softened. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, add more oil and sauté the zucchini until slightly browned. Remove and set aside.
Add the onions and bell peppers. Cook for 8-10 minutes until they are soft and the onions are translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and Herbes de Provence. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the crushed tomatoes and return the eggplant and zucchini to the pot. Stir well to combine.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30-40 minutes. The vegetables should be tender but not mushy, and the sauce should be thick.
Season with salt and pepper. Drizzle with a little fresh olive oil and garnish with torn basil leaves before serving warm or at room temperature.

name: Coq au Vin
steps: In a large Dutch oven, cook the bacon lardons over medium heat until crispy. Remove the bacon and leave the rendered fat in the pot.
Season chicken quarters with salt and pepper. Sear them in the bacon fat until the skin is deep golden brown. Remove and set aside.
Add the pearl onions and mushrooms to the pot. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Stir in the tomato paste and garlic, cooking for 1 minute. Pour in the red wine and chicken stock, scraping the bottom of the pot to release the fond.
Return the chicken and bacon to the pot. Add the thyme sprigs. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low.
Cover and simmer gently for 60-70 minutes until the chicken is tender and falling off the bone.
About 15 minutes before finishing, add the onions and mushrooms back to the sauce. If the sauce is too thin, simmer uncovered for the last 10 minutes to reduce.

name: Croque Monsieur
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (400°F). Lightly butter one side of each bread slice.
Place the slices on a baking sheet, butter-side up, and toast in the oven for 3 minutes until lightly golden.
Remove and spread a thin layer of Dijon mustard on the toasted side of two slices. Top with ham and half of the grated Gruyère.
Place the other two bread slices on top (toasted side down) to close the sandwiches.
Generously spread the Béchamel sauce over the top of the sandwiches, covering them completely to the edges.
Sprinkle the remaining Gruyère cheese over the Béchamel layer.
Bake for 10-12 minutes, then switch to the broiler for 2 minutes until the cheese is bubbly, brown, and scorched in spots. Serve hot.

name: French Onion Soup
steps: In a large pot, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the sliced onions and a pinch of salt.
Cook the onions slowly, stirring occasionally, for 45-50 minutes. They should become deep mahogany brown and jammy. Do not rush this step; it is the source of the flavor.
Deglaze the pot with white wine, scraping the bottom to release the browned bits.
Add the beef broth and thyme. Bring to a simmer and cook for another 20 minutes to meld the flavors.
While the soup simmers, toast the baguette slices in the oven until very crisp. Rub one side of each toast with a raw garlic clove.
Ladle the hot soup into oven-safe crocks. Place one or two baguette slices on top of each.
Cover generously with shredded Gruyère. Broil in the oven for 3-5 minutes until the cheese is melted and deeply browned. Serve immediately.

name: Crepes
steps: In a large blender or mixing bowl, combine flour, sugar, eggs, milk, melted butter, and vanilla. Blend until completely smooth with no lumps.
CRITICAL STEP: Let the batter rest in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes. This allows the flour to fully hydrate and the air bubbles to settle.
Heat a 10-inch non-stick crepe pan over medium heat. Lightly grease with a tiny bit of butter or oil.
Pour about 1/4 cup of batter into the center of the pan and immediately tilt and swirl the pan to coat the bottom in a very thin, even layer.
Cook for 1-2 minutes until the edges are lacy and light brown, and the top looks dry.
Carefully flip using a thin spatula or your fingers and cook the other side for 30 seconds.
Stack the crepes on a plate and cover with a towel to keep them soft. Fill with chocolate spread, fruit, or lemon and sugar.

name: Grilled Chicken Bowl
steps: Marinate the chicken in olive oil, dried oregano, and lemon juice for 20 minutes.
Heat a grill pan over medium-high heat. Grill the chicken for 6-7 minutes per side until fully cooked. Let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing into strips.
Prepare your bowl base by adding the warm cooked quinoa.
Arrange the tomatoes, cucumbers, olives, and a large scoop of hummus in separate sections on top of the quinoa.
Add the sliced grilled chicken to the center of the bowl.
Sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese over the vegetables.
Drizzle everything with a little extra virgin olive oil and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice before serving.

name: Falafel
steps: IMPORTANT: Use dried chickpeas that have been soaked for 24 hours. Canned chickpeas are too soft and will cause the falafel to fall apart.
Place the soaked (and drained) chickpeas, onion, garlic, parsley, cilantro, and cumin into a food processor.
Pulse until the mixture is finely ground but still has a slightly gritty texture (like coarse sand). Do not over-process into a paste.
Transfer to a bowl and stir in the flour and a pinch of salt. Chill the mixture in the fridge for 30 minutes to help it firm up.
Scoop about 2 tablespoons of the mixture and form into small balls or slightly flattened patties.
Heat 2 inches of oil in a deep skillet to 175°C (350°F). Fry the falafel in batches for 3-4 minutes, turning once, until they are deep brown and crispy on the outside.
Serve warm inside a pita bread with tahini sauce and fresh salad.

name: Hummus
steps: For extra smooth hummus, simmer the canned chickpeas in water with 1/2 tsp baking soda for 15 minutes, then rinse and rub off the skins (optional but recommended).
Place the tahini and lemon juice in the food processor and process for 1 minute until creamy and pale.
Add the chickpeas, garlic, and salt. Process for 2-3 minutes, scraping down the sides occasionally, until very thick and somewhat smooth.
While the processor is running, drizzle in the ice water. This is the secret to a fluffy, light texture.
Continue processing until the hummus is perfectly silky and smooth.
Transfer to a serving bowl. Use a spoon to create a spiral indentation on the surface.
Fill the spiral with a generous amount of olive oil and a sprinkle of smoked paprika. Serve with warm pita bread.

name: Greek Salad
steps: Note: Authentic Greek salad does not contain lettuce. Start by placing the cucumber slices and tomato wedges in a large salad bowl.
Add the thinly sliced red onions and whole Kalamata olives.
Drizzle with the red wine vinegar and extra virgin olive oil.
Gently toss the vegetables to coat them in the dressing.
Instead of crumbling the cheese, place the whole slab of feta directly on top of the vegetables.
Sprinkle the entire salad—especially the feta—liberally with dried oregano.
Finish with a final small drizzle of olive oil. Serve immediately at room temperature with crusty bread to soak up the juices.

name: Shawarma
steps: In a bowl, combine yogurt, shawarma spices, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt. Toss with chicken thighs and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a large skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat. Sear the chicken for 6-8 minutes per side until charred and cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the heat and let it rest for 5 minutes. Slice into thin, vertical strips against the grain.
Warm the pita bread slightly in the pan or oven to make it flexible.
Spread a generous layer of garlic sauce (Toum) in the center of the pita. Add the sliced chicken, pickles, and tomatoes.
Drizzle with a little tahini sauce.
Fold the bottom up and roll the pita tightly. Wrap the bottom half in foil or parchment paper to keep it secure and serve hot.

name: Couscous Bowl
steps: Bring the vegetable broth to a boil in a small pot. Stir in the dry couscous, cover with a lid, and immediately remove from heat.
Let the couscous sit for 5-10 minutes to absorb the liquid, then fluff it thoroughly with a fork.
While the couscous is resting, roast the zucchini and bell peppers in a 200°C oven or sauté them in a pan until tender and browned.
In a large serving bowl, assemble the base with the fluffed couscous.
Arrange the roasted vegetables and chickpeas neatly on top.
Sprinkle with crumbled feta cheese and freshly chopped parsley.
Drizzle the lemon vinaigrette over the bowl and serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Chicken Shawarma
steps: Whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, cumin, turmeric, coriander, and garlic powder in a mixing bowl.
Add the thinly sliced chicken and marinate for 20 minutes.
Heat a large skillet over high heat. Sear the chicken strips in a single layer until they are golden-brown and crispy on the edges (about 4-5 minutes).
Serve the sliced chicken over a bed of yellow basmati rice.
Add a side of hummus, a small salad (cucumbers and tomatoes), and a drizzle of white garlic sauce for a traditional platter presentation.

name: Lamb Kofta
steps: In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, grated onion, minced garlic, parsley, and all the spices.
Knead the mixture by hand for 3-5 minutes. This helps the proteins bind so the meat stays on the skewers.
Take a handful of the meat and mold it around a metal or soaked wooden skewer, creating a long, sausage-like shape.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Lightly brush with oil.
Grill the koftas for 3-4 minutes per side (about 12 minutes total) until well-charred and cooked through.
Serve hot with pita bread, tzatziki, and sliced red onions.

name: Hummus Plate
steps: Spread the hummus in a circular motion on a large, flat plate, creating a shallow well in the center.
Place the warm whole chickpeas in the central well.
Drizzle a generous amount of high-quality olive oil into the center and over the edges of the hummus.
Dust the top with a pinch of smoked paprika and cumin.
Garnish with toasted pine nuts and freshly chopped parsley.
Serve with warm pita bread or crunchy pita chips for dipping.

name: Falafel Wrap
steps: Warm the pita bread until soft and pliable.
Spread a thin layer of hummus on the lower half of the pita.
Place the warm, crispy falafel balls in a row and lightly crush them with a fork to help them stay in the wrap.
Top with the diced tomato and cucumber salad and the pickled turnips.
Drizzle generously with tahini sauce.
Roll the pita tightly, ensuring the filling is packed well. Serve immediately while the falafel is still crunchy.

name: Tabbouleh
steps: Rinse the bulgur wheat and soak it in the lemon juice and olive oil mixture for 20 minutes until softened.
Note: Tabbouleh is a parsley salad, not a grain salad. The parsley must be washed, dried completely, and chopped by hand with a very sharp knife to avoid bruising.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped parsley, mint, and green onions.
Add the finely diced tomatoes (strain away excess juice before adding).
Add the soaked bulgur and any remaining dressing to the bowl.
Toss gently and season with salt. Serve chilled or at room temperature with romaine lettuce leaves as 'scoops'.

name: Rice Pilaf
steps: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain well.
In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the broken vermicelli pasta bits. Sauté for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the pasta is a deep golden brown.
Add the rice to the pan and stir to coat every grain in butter.
Pour in the broth and add salt. Bring to a rolling boil.
Reduce heat to the lowest setting, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-18 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the rice sit undisturbed for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork and serve.

name: Quiche Lorraine
steps: Preheat your oven to 190°C (375°F). Line a tart pan with the shortcrust pastry and blind bake for 10 minutes using pie weights.
In a skillet, fry the bacon lardons until crispy. Drain the excess fat and spread the bacon evenly across the bottom of the pre-baked crust.
In a mixing bowl, whisk together the eggs, heavy cream, milk, nutmeg, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
If using, sprinkle the shredded Gruyère cheese over the bacon.
Carefully pour the egg and cream mixture over the bacon and cheese.
Bake for 30-35 minutes until the custard is set and the top is lightly golden-brown.
Let the quiche cool for at least 15 minutes before slicing; this allows the custard to firm up properly.

name: Beef Bourguignon
steps: In a large Dutch oven, fry the bacon lardons until crispy. Remove bacon and keep the fat.
Pat the beef cubes dry with paper towels. Working in batches, sear the beef in the bacon fat over high heat until deeply browned on all sides. Remove beef.
Sauté the carrots and onions in the same pot for 5 minutes.
Add the tomato paste and garlic, stir for 1 minute, then add the beef and bacon back to the pot.
Pour in the entire bottle of wine and the beef stock. Add a bouquet garni (thyme, bay leaf).
Cover and simmer on very low heat for 2.5 to 3 hours until the beef is fork-tender.
In the last 20 minutes, sauté the mushrooms in butter and add them to the stew. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

name: Paella
steps: In a wide, shallow paella pan, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Brown the chicken pieces and chorizo slices until golden. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, sauté diced onions, garlic, and bell peppers (sofrito) until soft and fragrant.
Add the rice to the pan and stir for 2 minutes until translucent. Sprinkle with smoked paprika and salt.
Pour in the hot chicken broth infused with saffron. Stir once to distribute the rice evenly, then DO NOT STIR AGAIN. This is crucial for forming the 'socarrat' (crusty bottom).
Simmer over medium-low heat for 15 minutes. Once the liquid is mostly absorbed, arrange the shrimp and peas on top of the rice.
Continue cooking for 8-10 minutes until the shrimp are pink and the liquid is completely gone. You should hear a light crackling sound from the bottom.
Cover the pan with a clean towel and let it rest for 5 minutes. Serve with fresh lemon wedges.
equipment: Paella Pan or Large Wide Skillet

name: Tortilla Española
steps: Heat the olive oil in a non-stick skillet. Add the sliced potatoes and onions. The oil should almost cover them. Cook on medium-low heat, occasionally stirring, until the potatoes are tender but not browned (about 15-20 mins).
Drain the potatoes and onions in a colander, reserving 2 tablespoons of the oil. Let them cool slightly.
In a large bowl, whisk the eggs with salt. Fold in the cooked potatoes and onions. Let the mixture sit for 10 minutes so the potatoes absorb the egg.
Heat the reserved oil in the skillet over medium-high heat. Pour in the egg mixture and spread it evenly.
Reduce heat to medium. Cook for 5 minutes, using a spatula to shape the edges into a curve.
Place a large, flat plate over the skillet. Carefully and quickly flip the skillet to transfer the tortilla onto the plate.
Slide the tortilla back into the pan (uncooked side down) and cook for another 3-4 minutes until golden and set. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Patatas Bravas
steps: Peel the potatoes and cut them into 2cm cubes. Soak in cold water for 15 minutes, then pat dry thoroughly.
Heat oil in a deep fryer or heavy pot to 180°C. Fry the potatoes in batches until they are deep golden-brown and very crispy. Drain on paper towels.
Prepare the Brava sauce: In a small pan, simmer tomato puree, smoked paprika, cayenne, and a little salt until thickened and dark red.
Prepare the Alioli by mixing mayonnaise with minced garlic and a squeeze of lemon.
Place the hot potatoes on a platter. Pour the spicy tomato sauce over them.
Top with generous dollops of garlic alioli and serve hot with toothpicks.

name: Garlic Shrimp
steps: Pat the shrimp completely dry with paper towels. Season lightly with salt.
In a small cast-iron skillet or clay pot (cazuela), combine olive oil, sliced garlic, and chili flakes.
Turn heat to medium and cook until the garlic starts to dance in the oil and turns light golden-brown. Do not burn the garlic.
Increase heat to high and add the shrimp. Cook for 1-2 minutes per side until they just turn pink and opaque.
Remove immediately from the heat; the residual heat of the oil will finish the cooking.
Sprinkle with fresh parsley and serve while the oil is still sizzling. Serve with plenty of crusty bread to dip into the garlic oil.
equipment: Small Cast Iron Skillet or Cazuela

name: Churros
steps: In a saucepan, bring water, butter, and a pinch of salt to a rolling boil.
Remove from heat and dump in all the flour at once. Stir vigorously with a wooden spoon until a smooth dough ball forms and pulls away from the sides.
Let the dough cool for 5 minutes, then transfer to a piping bag fitted with a large star tip. The star tip is essential for the ridges and to prevent the churros from bursting in the oil.
Heat oil to 180°C. Pipe 10cm lengths of dough into the hot oil, cutting with scissors.
Fry for 2-3 minutes per side until deep golden brown. Drain on paper towels.
Immediately roll the hot churros in cinnamon sugar.
Melt the chocolate with heavy cream to make a thick dipping sauce and serve alongside.
equipment: Piping Bag with Large Star Tip

name: Gyro
steps: Season the meat strips with oregano, salt, pepper, and lemon juice. Sauté in a hot pan or grill until slightly charred and fully cooked.
Warm the pita bread on a dry skillet until soft and pliable.
Spread a thick layer of tzatziki down the center of the pita.
Layer the cooked meat, tomatoes, onions, and—if being traditional—a few hot french fries inside.
Fold the sides of the pita over the filling to form a cone shape.
Wrap the bottom in parchment paper or foil to hold it together and serve immediately.

name: Moussaka
steps: Brush eggplant slices with oil and bake or fry until golden. Set aside on paper towels.
Prepare the meat sauce: Brown the lamb with onions and garlic. Deglaze with red wine, then add tomato puree, cinnamon, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 20 minutes.
Prepare a thick Béchamel sauce (butter, flour, milk) and stir in a handful of cheese and a pinch of nutmeg.
Assemble: In a deep casserole dish, place a layer of eggplant. Cover with the meat sauce, then another layer of eggplant.
Pour the Béchamel sauce over the top, spreading it into a thick, even layer. Sprinkle with remaining cheese.
Bake at 180°C for 45-50 minutes until the top is deeply golden and the layers are set.
CRITICAL: Let the moussaka rest for at least 30 minutes before slicing to ensure the layers stay intact.

name: Souvlaki
steps: In a bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, and oregano.
Toss the chicken cubes in the marinade and let sit for at least 30 minutes in the fridge.
Thread the chicken pieces tightly onto the soaked wooden skewers.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Lightly oil the grates.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning every 3 minutes, until the chicken is charred on all sides and cooked through.
Serve hot with pita bread, tzatziki, and lemon wedges.

name: Tzatziki
steps: Grate the cucumber using a box grater. Place the grated cucumber in a clean kitchen towel and squeeze as hard as possible to remove every drop of excess water.
In a mixing bowl, combine the thick Greek yogurt with the squeezed cucumber.
Stir in the minced garlic, chopped dill, and lemon juice.
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt to taste.
Let the tzatziki sit in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving. This allows the garlic and dill to infuse into the yogurt properly.

name: Jerk Chicken
steps: Poke deep holes into the chicken with a fork to allow the marinade to penetrate. Rub the chicken generously with jerk seasoning, scotch bonnet, thyme, allspice, sugar, and lime juice.
Marinate in the fridge for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.
Preheat your grill to medium heat. If using a BBQ, use pimento wood chips for authentic smoke.
Place the chicken on the grill skin-side down. Cook for 40-45 minutes, turning every 10 minutes.
Baste the chicken with any leftover marinade during the first 20 minutes of cooking (do not baste with raw marinade near the end).
Ensure the chicken is charred on the outside and reaches an internal temperature of 75°C.
Serve with Rice and Peas and fried plantains.

name: Rice and Peas
steps: Rinse the rice in cold water until the water runs clear to remove excess starch. Drain well.
In a large heavy-bottomed pot, combine the coconut milk, the liquid from the canned kidney beans, and enough water to reach 3.5 cups of total liquid.
Add the kidney beans, bruised scallions, garlic, allspice berries, and thyme sprigs. Bring the mixture to a boil.
Add the rice and the whole Scotch Bonnet pepper. IMPORTANT: Do not pierce or burst the pepper, or the dish will be extremely spicy. It is meant to provide aroma, not heat.
Once the liquid level drops to just above the rice, reduce the heat to the lowest setting and cover with a tight-fitting lid.
Steam for 18-20 minutes until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Remove the thyme, scallions, and the whole pepper carefully. Fluff the rice with a fork to distribute the beans evenly before serving.

name: Curry Chicken
steps: Season the chicken with 2 tablespoons of curry powder, salt, and pepper. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes (or overnight) for maximum flavor.
Heat oil in a large pot. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of curry powder to the hot oil and 'burn' it for 30 seconds to release the oils and deepen the color.
Add the chicken pieces and brown them on all sides.
Add the onions, garlic, and minced scotch bonnet. Sauté for 3 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the potatoes, carrots, and thyme. Pour in the coconut milk and just enough water to almost cover the chicken.
Bring to a boil, then simmer on medium-low heat for 30 minutes until the chicken is tender and the sauce has thickened into a rich gravy.
Serve hot with white rice or Jamaican hard dough bread.

name: Fried Plantains
steps: Peel the plantains by cutting off the ends and slicing through the skin lengthwise.
Slice the plantains diagonally into 1.5 cm thick ovals. Diagonal cuts provide more surface area for caramelization.
Heat about 1 cm of oil in a frying pan over medium heat.
Place the slices in the hot oil. Fry for 2-3 minutes per side. Because they are ripe, the natural sugars will caramelize quickly and turn the edges dark brown.
Remove when they are golden-brown and soft in the middle.
Drain on paper towels and sprinkle with a tiny pinch of salt to balance the sweetness. Serve immediately.

name: Oxtail
steps: Trim excess fat from the oxtail. Season with browning sauce, allspice, ginger, garlic, and salt. Let it marinate for at least 2 hours.
In a heavy pot or pressure cooker, sear the oxtail pieces until deeply browned and caramelized on all sides.
Add the beef stock, thyme, and the whole scotch bonnet pepper. Bring to a boil.
If using a heavy pot, simmer on low heat for 2.5 to 3 hours. If using a pressure cooker, cook for 45-50 minutes until the meat is fork-tender and falling off the bone.
Add the canned butter beans and their liquid to the pot. Simmer uncovered for the final 15 minutes to allow the sauce to thicken into a glossy, dark gravy.
Remove the thyme sprigs and the whole pepper before serving with Rice and Peas.
equipment: Pressure Cooker or Heavy Dutch Oven

name: Jollof Rice
steps: Blend the tomatoes, red bell peppers, and scotch bonnets until smooth. Boil this mixture in a pot until it reduces to a thick paste.
In a separate large heavy pot, heat vegetable oil and fry sliced onions until golden. Add the tomato paste and fry for 5 minutes, stirring constantly to remove the sour taste.
Pour in the reduced pepper-tomato mixture. Fry together (the 'stew' base) for 10 minutes until the oil separates from the sauce.
Add curry powder, thyme, bay leaves, and chicken stock. Season generously with salt.
Add the washed parboiled rice. The liquid should be just level with the rice. Cover the pot tightly with aluminum foil, then the lid, to trap every bit of steam.
Cook on very low heat for 30 minutes. Do not open the lid frequently.
Once the rice is tender and the liquid is gone, turn up the heat for 2 minutes to slightly burn the bottom (this creates the 'party jollof' smoky flavor). Fluff and serve.
equipment: Large Heavy-Bottomed Pot with Tight Lid

name: Suya
steps: Slice the beef into very thin strips, ideally 1mm thick (freezing the meat for 30 mins helps with thin slicing).
Thread the beef strips onto skewers in a zig-zag pattern so they lay flat.
In a shallow dish, combine the Yaji spice, cayenne, and ginger. Coat the skewered meat heavily with the spice mix, pressing it into the meat.
Drizzle or brush the meat lightly with vegetable oil.
Preheat a grill to high heat. Grill the skewers for 3-4 minutes per side. The thin meat will cook quickly and the peanut spices will form a dry, savory crust.
Serve hot with extra Yaji spice, sliced raw red onions, and chunks of cucumber or tomato to balance the heat.

name: Egusi Soup
steps: Mix the ground egusi seeds with a little water or broth to form a thick, gritty paste.
Heat the palm oil in a pot over medium heat for 2 minutes (do not bleach it). Add diced onions and the locust beans (Iru).
Scoop the egusi paste into the oil in small lumps. Fry for 5-8 minutes, stirring gently. The egusi should form small, solid curd-like lumps.
Add the pre-cooked meat, beef broth, and ground crayfish. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes.
Add the chopped spinach. Stir well and cook for 5 more minutes until the leaves are wilted but still bright green.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve with Pounded Yam or Eba (Garri).

name: Fried Rice (West African style)
steps: In a large wok or deep skillet, heat oil over high heat. Sauté the finely diced liver or shrimp until fully cooked. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add the mixed vegetables and green bell peppers. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Add the curry powder, thyme, and bouillon cube to the vegetables. Stir for 1 minute until the oil turns yellow and fragrant.
Add the cold cooked rice to the pan in batches. Stir-fry vigorously to ensure every grain of rice is coated in the yellow curry oil.
Add the meat/shrimp back into the pan. Continue stir-frying for 5 minutes until the rice is steaming hot and evenly colored.
Serve with a side of fried plantains and grilled chicken.

name: Grilled Fish
steps: Scale and clean the fish. Score the skin by making 3-4 deep diagonal cuts on each side of the fish.
In a small bowl, mix the ginger, garlic, minced scotch bonnet, lemon juice, oil, and salt to create a thick paste.
Rub the paste all over the fish, ensuring it gets deep into the scores and the cavity of the fish. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat a grill or oven to 200°C. If grilling, oil the grates well to prevent sticking.
Grill the fish for 12-15 minutes per side. Baste with any remaining marinade halfway through.
The fish is done when the skin is charred and crispy, and the flesh flakes easily with a fork.
Serve with a side of fried yams, plantains, or Attieke.

name: Plantain
steps: Cut off the ends of the plantains and peel them.
Slice the plantains into rounds or diagonal slices (about 1 cm thick).
Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium heat (about 2 cm deep).
Fry the plantain slices until they are golden brown on the first side (about 3 minutes).
Flip and fry the other side until evenly browned and caramelized.
Drain on a paper-towel-lined plate. Sprinkle with a tiny pinch of salt while hot. Serve as a side to Jollof rice, beans, or fried rice.

name: Turkey Lettuce Wraps
steps: In a large skillet over medium-high heat, add a teaspoon of oil and brown the ground turkey. Break it into small crumbles as it cooks until no pink remains.
Add the minced garlic, ginger, and diced water chestnuts to the pan. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the aromatics are fragrant.
Stir in the hoisin sauce and soy sauce. Toss the meat thoroughly to ensure even coating and cook for another 2 minutes until the sauce glazes the turkey.
While the meat finishes, wash the butter lettuce leaves carefully and pat them dry. They should be chilled and crisp.
Stir half of the sliced green onions into the turkey mixture and remove from heat.
To serve, spoon 2-3 tablespoons of the turkey mixture into the center of each lettuce leaf. Garnish with the remaining green onions and a squeeze of lime if desired.

name: Garlic Shrimp with Vegetables
steps: Pat the shrimp dry with paper towels and season them lightly with salt, pepper, and dried oregano.
Heat olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the bell peppers and zucchini.
Sauté the vegetables for 4-5 minutes until they are tender-crisp and have slight char marks.
Push the vegetables to the side of the pan. Add the sliced garlic and shrimp to the empty space.
Cook the shrimp for 1.5 to 2 minutes per side until they turn pink and opaque. Do not overcook as they will become rubbery.
Toss the shrimp and vegetables together, then drizzle with fresh lemon juice and remove from heat immediately. Serve hot.

name: Baked Salmon with Asparagus
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (400°F). Line a large rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper for easy cleanup.
Place the salmon fillet in the center of the sheet and arrange the trimmed asparagus spears around it in a single layer.
Drizzle both the salmon and asparagus with olive oil. Season with garlic powder, dried dill, salt, and pepper.
Place the lemon slices on top of the salmon fillet to infuse flavor and keep the meat moist while baking.
Bake for 12-15 minutes, depending on the thickness of the salmon. The fish is done when it flakes easily with a fork and the asparagus is tender-crisp.
Serve immediately, using the roasted lemon slices for an extra squeeze of juice over the dish.

name: Chicken Stir-Fry
steps: Whisk together the soy sauce, grated ginger, honey, and cornstarch in a small bowl to create the stir-fry sauce.
Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat with a light spray of oil. Add the chicken slices and sear for 3-4 minutes until golden and cooked through. Remove and set aside.
Add the broccoli and bell peppers to the hot wok with a tablespoon of water. Steam-fry for 3 minutes by stirring constantly until vibrant and tender.
Return the chicken to the wok. Pour the sauce over the ingredients.
Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens into a glossy glaze that coats the chicken and vegetables evenly. Serve hot on its own or with cauliflower rice.

name: Greek Yogurt Chicken Salad
steps: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the Greek yogurt, Dijon mustard, chopped herbs, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Add the shredded cooked chicken (rotisserie chicken works great here) to the bowl.
Fold in the diced celery and halved red grapes. The grapes provide a natural sweetness that balances the tanginess of the yogurt.
Mix well until all ingredients are evenly coated in the dressing.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve chilled over a bed of mixed greens or inside large butter lettuce cups for a low-carb lunch.

name: Egg White Omelet
steps: Whisk the egg whites in a small bowl until they are slightly frothy.
Spray a small non-stick skillet with cooking spray and heat over medium-low heat.
Add the spinach and green onions to the pan. Sauté for 1-2 minutes until the spinach is just wilted. Remove the vegetables from the pan.
Pour the egg whites into the skillet. Let them cook undisturbed for 2 minutes until the edges are set and the bottom is white.
Spoon the sautéed spinach and crumbled feta cheese onto one half of the omelet.
Carefully fold the other half over the filling using a silicone spatula. Cook for another 30-60 seconds to melt the cheese slightly. Slide onto a plate and serve immediately.

name: Chocolate Chip Cookies
steps: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Line two large baking sheets with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, cream together the softened butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar until the mixture is light and fluffy.
Beat in the egg and vanilla extract until well combined.
Gradually stir in the flour and a pinch of salt until a dough forms. Do not overmix.
Fold in the chocolate chips by hand.
Drop rounded tablespoons of dough (or use a cookie scoop) onto the prepared baking sheets, spaced 2 inches apart.
Bake for 10-12 minutes until the edges are golden-brown but the centers are still slightly soft. Let them cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack.

name: Brownies
steps: Preheat your oven to 170°C (325°F). Grease an 8x8 inch square baking pan and line it with parchment paper, leaving an overhang on two sides.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the melted butter, sugar, and cocoa powder until well combined.
Add the eggs one at a time, whisking vigorously after each addition until the batter is glossy.
Stir in the vanilla extract.
Gently fold in the flour and a pinch of salt using a spatula until just combined. The batter will be thick.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top.
Bake for 20-25 minutes. A toothpick inserted into the center should come out with a few moist crumbs. Do not overbake. Let cool completely in the pan before lifting out and slicing into squares.

name: Cheesecake Cups
steps: In a medium bowl, beat the softened cream cheese, sugar, and vanilla extract until completely smooth and creamy.
In a separate bowl, whip the heavy cream until stiff peaks form, then gently fold it into the cream cheese mixture to create a light mousse texture.
Spoon 2 tablespoons of graham cracker crumbs into the bottom of 4-6 small glass cups or ramekins.
Pipe or spoon the cheesecake filling over the crumbs, filling each cup about 3/4 full.
Smooth the tops and refrigerate for at least 2 hours (or overnight) to allow the filling to set.
Garnish with fresh berries or a drizzle of fruit coulis just before serving.

name: Banana Bread
steps: Preheat your oven to 175°C (350°F). Grease a 9x5 inch loaf pan thoroughly with butter or cooking spray.
In a large bowl, mash the ripe bananas with a fork until mostly smooth.
Stir the melted butter into the mashed bananas.
Mix in the beaten egg, sugar, and a pinch of salt.
Sprinkle the baking soda over the mixture and stir it in. Finally, gently fold in the flour until no white streaks remain.
Pour the batter into the prepared loaf pan.
Bake for 55-65 minutes. The bread is done when a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Let it cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack to cool completely.

name: Strawberry Shortcake
steps: Place the sliced strawberries in a bowl and sprinkle with 1 tablespoon of sugar. Let them sit for 15-30 minutes at room temperature (macerating) until they release their natural juices.
In a chilled mixing bowl, combine the heavy cream, the remaining 2 tablespoons of sugar, and vanilla extract.
Whisk by hand or with an electric mixer until medium-stiff peaks form. Do not overbeat, or it will turn into butter.
If using large shortcakes, slice them in half horizontally. If using small individual cups, leave them whole.
Place the bottom half of the shortcake on a serving plate. Spoon a generous amount of the macerated strawberries and their syrup over the cake.
Top with a large dollop of the freshly whipped cream.
Place the top half of the shortcake over the cream and finish with one final small dollop of cream and a whole strawberry for garnish.

name: Chocolate Mug Cake
steps: Find a large microwave-safe ceramic mug (at least 12 oz) to prevent the batter from overflowing.
Add the flour, sugar, cocoa powder, and baking powder directly into the mug. Whisk with a small fork until the dry ingredients are well combined.
Pour in the milk and vegetable oil. Stir vigorously until the batter is smooth and no flour pockets remain at the bottom of the mug.
Stir in the chocolate chips if using; these will create a molten center.
Place the mug in the center of the microwave and cook on high for 90 seconds. The cake should rise and look set on top.
Let the cake rest in the microwave for 1 minute before removing. The center will be very hot. Top with a scoop of vanilla ice cream or a dust of powdered sugar.

name: Fruit Yogurt Parfait
steps: Wash and thoroughly dry the fresh berries. If using strawberries, hull and slice them into bite-sized pieces.
Select a tall glass or a clear glass jar to show off the layers.
Spoon 1/3 of the yogurt into the bottom of the glass.
Add a layer of berries, followed by a sprinkle of granola. The granola provides a necessary crunch against the soft yogurt.
Repeat the layers once or twice more until the glass is full, finishing with a layer of berries on top.
Drizzle the honey over the final layer of berries and garnish with a fresh mint leaf. Serve immediately while the granola is still crunchy.

name: Garlic Butter Shrimp
steps: Pat the shrimp dry with paper towels. Season lightly with salt and a pinch of chili flakes.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium-high heat until it starts to foam.
Add the minced garlic and cook for about 1 minute. Watch closely to ensure the garlic turns golden but doesn't burn.
