Roast at 200°C for 25 minutes. Every 10 minutes, give the tray a shake.
The vegetables are done when the edges are charred and the centers are tender.
Remove herb sprigs and serve as the perfect nutritious side.

name: Grilled Vegetables
steps: Slice the zucchini and eggplant into 1cm thick rounds. Cut the pepper into large flats.
Brush both sides of the vegetables generously with olive oil and salt.
Heat a grill pan over high heat. Grill the vegetables for 3-4 minutes per side until prominent char marks appear.
Arrange on a platter while hot.
Drizzle with cold balsamic glaze right before serving to add acidity and sweetness.

name: Garlic Bread
steps: Slice the bread in half lengthwise. In a bowl, mix butter, garlic, and oregano.
Spread the garlic butter heavily on both cut sides of the bread.
Sprinkle with parmesan cheese.
Broil at 220°C for 5-8 minutes until the edges are dark brown and the butter has soaked into the center.
Slice into 2-inch pieces and serve while the butter is still bubbling.

name: Side Salad
steps: Spin the lettuce until dry. Combine with cucumber in a chilled bowl.
Add the dressing only right before serving to avoid wilting.
Toss gently with two spoons.
Designed as a palate cleanser to accompany heavy mains.

name: Rice Side (Herbed Basmati)
steps: Boil rice in 1.5 cups of water until fluffy.
Stir in a knob of butter and fresh parsley.
A simple, neutral side for stews and grilled meats.

name: Beans Side (Savory Black Beans)
steps: Simmer the beans in their own liquid with spices for 10 minutes.
Mash a small portion of the beans with a fork to thicken the sauce.
Garnish with cilantro. Perfect side for rice bowls or tacos.

name: Corn Side (Creamed Corn)
steps: Sauté corn in butter for 5 minutes until sweet.
Pour in cream and nutmeg. Simmer until the cream reduces by half and coats the corn.
Season with salt and lots of black pepper. A classic comfort side dish.

name: Snack Plate (Mezze Spread)
steps: Arrange the cold components (feta, olives, cucumber) in distinct sections on a plate.
Place the hummus in a small central ramekin with a drizzle of oil.
Warm the pita wedges and place them around the edges.
Designed for sharing or a high-protein afternoon snack.

name: Protein Snack (Power Duo)
steps: Hard boil the egg for exactly 10 minutes. Shock in ice water to make peeling easy.
Place the peeled egg in a portable container.
Add the almonds and turkey jerky.
High protein and low carb snack to keep blood sugar stable throughout the day.

name: Healthy Snack
steps: Core and slice the apple into uniform wedges. Dip them briefly in lemon water to prevent browning if not eating immediately.
Place the apple wedges on a plate.
Drizzle or spread a teaspoon of peanut butter over each slice.
Sprinkle with hemp seeds for added protein and Omega-3s, and finish with a few dark chocolate chips for a healthy antioxidant boost.

name: Fruit Snack
steps: Peel the mango and slice the flesh into long, thick spears.
Arrange the spears in a cup or on a small plate.
Squeeze fresh lime juice over the mango to activate the flavors.
Dust generously with Tajín (chili-lime salt). The contrast between the sweet mango and the spicy, salty seasoning is the hallmark of this traditional snack.

name: Veggie Snack
steps: Wash and dry the celery and carrots. Cut them into 4-inch sticks.
Fill the hollow center of the celery stalks with hummus or light cream cheese.
Top the celery with raisins (classic 'Ants on a Log').
Serve alongside the carrot sticks for a high-fiber, low-glycemic snack that provides sustained energy.

name: Dips and Sides
steps: Arrange three small ramekins on a large platter.
Fill each with one of the dips (chickpea, yogurt-cucumber, and roasted eggplant).
Drizzle the hummus and baba ganoush with extra virgin olive oil and a dash of paprika.
Place the raw vegetable sticks and warm pita bread around the dips.
This 'trio' approach ensures a variety of probiotics, healthy fats, and vitamins in one snack.

name: Loaded Fries
steps: Double-fry the potatoes at 150°C and then 190°C to ensure the interior is fluffy and the exterior can support the heavy toppings.
Drain the fries and salt them while piping hot.
Place the fries in a shallow bowl or on a small baking sheet.
Pour hot cheddar cheese sauce evenly over the fries.
Top with the crispy bacon, sliced jalapeños, and green onions.
Add a final dollop of cold sour cream in the center to balance the heat. Serve with a fork.

name: Quick Snack
steps: Place the rice cake on a plate. Peel the string cheese into fine threads and lay them across the rice cake.
Assemble the nuts and dried apricots on the side.
This snack is designed for the '3-minute assembly' rule, providing healthy fats, protein, and natural sugars to prevent afternoon energy crashes.

name: Street Snack (Elote en Vaso)
steps: Steam or boil the corn kernels until hot and tender.
Fill a tall glass or cup halfway with corn.
Add half of the mayonnaise and a squeeze of lime; stir well to coat the warm corn.
Fill the rest of the cup with corn.
Top with the remaining mayo, a heavy dusting of Cotija cheese, and chili powder.
Serve with a spoon. This 'Corn in a Cup' style is the most popular way to eat Elote as a handheld street snack.

name: Chocolate Cake
steps: Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F). Grease and flour two 8-inch cake pans.
Whisk dry ingredients (flour, sugar, cocoa, baking soda) in a large bowl.
Add eggs, buttermilk, and oil; beat until smooth.
SECRET STEP: Stir in the hot coffee by hand. The heat 'blooms' the cocoa powder for a deeper chocolate flavor, and the thin batter ensures a moist crumb.
Bake for 30-35 minutes. Cool completely in the pans before frosting with chocolate ganache.

name: Vanilla Cake
steps: Creaming Method: Beat softened butter and sugar for 5 minutes until very pale and fluffy. This incorporates air for a light cake.
Add egg whites one at a time, beating well after each. Stir in the vanilla bean paste.
Alternate adding the flour and the milk, starting and ending with flour.
Divide batter between pans and bake at 180°C (350°F) for 25-30 minutes.
The use of egg whites only and cake flour results in a pure white, delicate sponge.

name: Ice Cream (Vanilla Bean Churn)
steps: Heat milk, half the sugar, and the vanilla bean/seeds in a pot until just simmering.
Whisk egg yolks and remaining sugar in a bowl until pale yellow.
TEMPERING: Slowly pour a ladle of hot milk into the yolks while whisking constantly, then return the entire mix to the pot.
Cook over low heat until the custard coats the back of a spoon (82°C). Do not boil.
Stir in the heavy cream. Chill the base in the fridge for at least 4 hours (overnight is best for texture).
Churn in an ice cream maker for 20 minutes, then freeze for 2 hours to 'ripen'.

name: Fruit Dessert (Apple Tarte Tatin)
steps: In an oven-proof skillet, melt butter and sugar over medium heat until a deep amber caramel forms.
Carefully arrange apple halves tightly in the skillet, cut-side up.
Cook on the stovetop for 15 minutes to soften the apples in the caramel.
Drape the puff pastry over the apples, tucking the edges down into the skillet.
Bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until the pastry is dark golden.
IMPORTANT: Let rest for 5 minutes, then invert onto a plate while the caramel is still liquid but starting to set. Serve warm with vanilla ice cream.

name: Cupcake (Red Velvet)
steps: Cream butter and sugar. Add the egg and the food coloring mixed with cocoa powder.
Add buttermilk and flour in alternating batches.
FINAL STEP: Mix baking soda and vinegar in a small cup (it will fizz) and immediately fold into the batter. This provides the chemical lift and traditional tang.
Bake at 175°C for 18-22 minutes.
Cool completely before piping with thick cream cheese frosting.

name: Pastry (Palmiers / Elephant Ears)
steps: Spread sugar and cinnamon heavily on your work surface. Lay the cold puff pastry on top.
Sprinkle more sugar on top of the pastry. Roll with a pin to press the sugar into the dough.
Fold the left and right sides toward the center until they meet in the middle, then fold one half over the other (like a book).
Slice into 1cm thick pieces. Place on a baking sheet, cut-side up.
Bake at 200°C for 12-15 minutes, flipping halfway through to caramelize the sugar on both sides.
The result should be 'shatter-crisp' and deeply caramelized.

name: Donuts (Classic Glazed)
steps: Knead a brioche-style dough (flour, yeast, milk, yolks, butter) for 10 minutes until elastic. Let rise for 1 hour.
Roll dough to 1.5cm thickness and cut out circles with a center hole.
SECOND RISE: Let the cut donuts rest for 45 minutes until they look puffy and light. This is the key to the 'white ring' in professional donuts.
Fry at 175°C for 90 seconds per side.
Dip into warm vanilla glaze immediately while the donut is still hot to ensure a thin, crackly shell.

name: Chocolate Dessert (Molten Lava Cake)
steps: Preheat oven to 220°C (425°F). Butter and cocoa powder the inside of two 6oz ramekins.
Melt chocolate and butter together until smooth.
In a bowl, whisk eggs, yolks, and sugar until thick and pale.
Fold the chocolate into the eggs, then gently stir in the flour.
Bake for exactly 11-13 minutes. The edges should be firm but the center should still jiggle slightly.
Let sit for 1 minute, then invert onto a plate. The center will flow out like lava when cut.

name: Layered Dessert (Strawberry Trifle)
steps: Prepare the jelly according to instructions and set it in the fridge until firm.
Place the sponge cake cubes at the bottom of a large glass trifle bowl and drizzle with sherry or juice.
Slice strawberries and press them against the glass sides for a decorative look.
Layer the set jelly over the cake, followed by a thick layer of cold vanilla custard.
Whip the cream to soft peaks and spread it as the final top layer.
Chill for at least 4 hours before serving to allow the layers to settle and the sponge to soften.

name: Baklava (Honey & Pistachio)
steps: Layer 10 sheets of phyllo in a buttered pan, brushing each layer generously with melted butter.
Spread half of the chopped nut mixture evenly over the pastry.
Add 5 more layers of buttered phyllo, the rest of the nuts, and finish with 10 more layers of phyllo.
Cut the baklava into diamond shapes with a very sharp knife BEFORE baking.
Bake at 165°C for 50 minutes until deep golden and crispy.
CRITICAL STEP: Immediately pour COLD honey-sugar syrup over the boiling HOT baklava. The 'sizzle' ensures the syrup is absorbed without making the pastry soggy.

name: Rice Pudding (Arroz con Leche)
steps: Simmer the rice in 2 cups of water with the cinnamon stick and lemon peel for 15 minutes until water is absorbed.
Slowly pour in the whole milk and simmer on low, stirring constantly to release the rice starch.
Once the mixture is creamy (about 25 mins), stir in the condensed milk for sweetness and extra richness.
Cook for another 5 minutes, then remove from heat and discard the peel and cinnamon stick.
Serve warm or chilled, topped with a heavy dusting of ground cinnamon.

name: Custard (Crème Brûlée)
steps: Heat cream and vanilla seeds in a pot until just simmering.
Whisk egg yolks and sugar until pale. Temper the eggs by slowly whisking in the hot cream.
Strain the custard and pour into individual ramekins.
Bake in a water bath (bain-marie) at 150°C for 35-40 minutes until set but still slightly wobbly in the center.
Chill for at least 4 hours. Before serving, sprinkle a thin layer of sugar on top.
Use a kitchen torch to caramelize the sugar into a hard, glass-like crust.

name: Pancake Dessert (Crêpes Suzette)
steps: In a wide skillet, melt butter and sugar until it starts to caramelize.
Add orange juice and zest; simmer until the sauce reduces and thickens.
Fold the crepes into triangles and place them in the bubbling orange sauce.
Pour the liqueur over the crepes and carefully ignite with a long match (flambé).
Baste the crepes with the flaming sauce until the alcohol evaporates. Serve immediately.

name: Waffle Dessert (Liège style)
steps: Fold the pearl sugar into the risen yeast dough by hand. The sugar pearls should be evenly distributed.
Heat a heavy-duty waffle iron to medium-high.
Place a ball of dough in the center. The pearl sugar will melt and caramelize on the surface, creating a crunchy glaze.
Cook for 4 minutes until dark golden brown.
Serve warm topped with whipped cream and a rich dark chocolate drizzle.

name: Healthy Dessert (Chia Berry Pudding)
steps: Whisk chia seeds, coconut milk, and maple syrup in a bowl.
Let sit for 10 minutes, then whisk again to prevent the seeds from clumping at the bottom.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours (or overnight).
Layer the set pudding in a glass with fresh mashed raspberries.
Top with a few whole berries and a mint leaf.

name: Vegan Dessert (Chocolate Avocado Mousse)
steps: Place the avocado flesh into a food processor.
Add cocoa powder, maple syrup, vanilla, and salt.
Process until the mixture is completely smooth and no green bits remain.
Chill for 1 hour to allow the texture to firm up and the flavors to meld.
Serve with a sprinkle of sea salt or fresh berries.

name: Keto Dessert (Almond Flour Brownies)
steps: Whisk eggs and erythritol until well combined. Stir in the melted butter.
Sift in the almond flour and cocoa powder.
Fold gently until a thick batter forms.
Bake in an 8x8 pan at 175°C for 20 minutes.
Let cool completely before slicing (Keto baked goods are very fragile while warm).

name: High Protein Dessert (Protein Cheesecake)
steps: Press the oat flour mixed with a little water into the bottom of a springform pan.
Blend yogurt, protein powder, egg whites, and stevia until smooth.
Pour over the crust and bake at 160°C for 45 minutes.
Turn off the oven and leave the door slightly ajar for 1 hour to prevent the top from cracking.
Chill for 6 hours before serving.

name: Middle Eastern Dessert (Kunafa)
steps: Finely shred the kataifi pastry and mix thoroughly with melted ghee.
Press half the pastry into a round baking pan.
Add a thick layer of desalted cheese in the center.
Top with the remaining pastry and bake at 190°C until the top is very crispy and golden.
Pour hot syrup over the hot kunafa immediately.
Garnish with ground pistachios and serve warm while the cheese is stretchy.

name: Asian Dessert (Mango Pomelo Sago)
steps: Boil sago for 10 minutes until translucent, then rinse in cold water.
Blend mango flesh with coconut milk and a little evaporated milk until smooth.
Stir the cooked sago into the mango mixture.
Chill for 2 hours.
Serve in bowls topped with more fresh mango cubes and the bitter-sweet pomelo segments.

name: Latin Dessert (Tres Leches Cake)
steps: Bake a light sponge cake and let it cool.
Use a fork to poke holes all over the surface of the cake.
Whisk the three milks (evaporated, condensed, cream) together.
Pour the milk mixture slowly over the cake. The holes allow it to soak through to the center.
Refrigerate overnight—this is the most important step for the correct texture.
Top with whipped cream and a sprinkle of cinnamon before serving.

name: Quick Dessert (Affogato)
steps: Place two scoops of premium vanilla gelato in a chilled glass.
Brew a fresh double shot of espresso.
Immediately pour the hot coffee over the cold gelato.
Top with crushed amaretti for texture and serve with a small spoon before it fully melts.

name: Easy Dessert (Fruit Galette)
steps: Roll out the pie crust into a rough circle on a baking sheet.
Toss the sliced fruit with sugar and cornstarch.
Pile the fruit in the center of the dough, leaving a 2-inch border.
Fold the edges of the dough over the fruit, pleating as you go to create a rustic border.
Brush the dough with egg wash and bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until golden.
Perfectly 'easy' because it requires no pie pan or precise crimping.

name: Traditional Dessert (Sticky Toffee Pudding)
steps: Soak dates in boiling water with baking soda until mushy, then blend into a paste.
Cream butter and sugar; add eggs and the date paste. Fold in flour.
Bake in individual molds at 180°C for 30 minutes.
Make the toffee sauce by boiling butter, brown sugar, and cream until dark and thick.
Poke holes in the warm cakes and pour half the sauce over them. Broil for 2 minutes to caramelize.
Serve with the remaining hot sauce and vanilla custard.

name: Street Food Dessert (Loukoumades)
steps: Allow the yeast dough to rise until it is bubbly and airy.
Heat oil to 180°C. Squeeze small balls of dough through your thumb and forefinger into the oil.
Fry until the 'donuts' are deep golden and float to the top.
Drain and immediately toss in warm honey syrup.
Sprinkle with lots of cinnamon and crushed walnuts. Eat with a toothpick.

name: Bistro Dessert (Profiteroles)
steps: Bake small balls of choux pastry at 200°C until puffed and hollow. Let them cool completely.
Slice the shells in half horizontally.
Place a large scoop of vanilla ice cream in the bottom half of each shell.
Place the top half back on.
Assemble three on a plate and pour piping hot chocolate sauce over the top so it melts the ice cream slightly.
Finish with toasted almonds.

name: Gourmet Dessert (Chocolate Soufflé)
steps: CRITICAL: Butter the ramekins with upward strokes and coat with sugar/cocoa. This helps the soufflé climb straight up.
Whisk egg whites and sugar to stiff, glossy peaks.
Fold 1/3 of the whites into the melted chocolate to lighten it.
Gently fold in the remaining whites, being careful not to deflate the air.
Fill ramekins to the brim and level off with a knife.
Bake at 190°C for 12-15 minutes. Serve immediately before the air cools and it collapses.

name: Fruit Tart (Tarte aux Fruits)
steps: Blind bake the tart shell until golden and crisp. Let cool.
Fill the shell with cold, silky pastry cream and smooth the surface.
Arrange the fruit in concentric circles (kiwi, strawberries, blueberries).
Brush the fruit with warm apricot glaze to provide a professional shine and prevent the fruit from drying out.
Chill for 1 hour before slicing.

name: Chocolate Mousse (Classic Dark)
steps: Melt the chocolate over a bain-marie. Let it cool to room temperature.
Whip the cream to soft peaks.
Whip egg whites and sugar to stiff peaks.
Fold the whipped cream into the chocolate, then gently fold in the egg whites.
The result should be light, airy, and full of bubbles.
Chill for at least 4 hours to set the structure.

name: Panna Cotta (Vanilla Bean)
steps: Soak gelatin sheets in cold water for 5 minutes (blooming).
Heat cream, sugar, and vanilla in a pot until the sugar dissolves. Do not boil.
Squeeze the water out of the gelatin and stir into the warm cream until dissolved.
Pour into molds and refrigerate for 6 hours.
To unmold, dip the bottom of the mold in hot water for 5 seconds.
Serve with a bright raspberry coulis to cut through the richness.

name: Tiramisu (Authentic Venetian)
steps: Whisk yolks and sugar over a pot of simmering water (Sabayon) until thick and pale. Let cool.
Gently fold the mascarpone into the egg mixture until smooth.
Dip each ladyfinger into the coffee-Marsala mix for only 1 second per side (do not soak).
Layer dipped cookies in a dish, cover with half the cream, and repeat.
Refrigerate for at least 12 hours to allow the coffee to migrate and the cream to set.
Dust heavily with cocoa powder right before serving.

name: Cheesecake (New York Style)
steps: Beat cream cheese and sugar until perfectly smooth. Add eggs one at a time on low speed.
Fold in sour cream and vanilla.
Wrap the outside of the springform pan in double foil to make it waterproof.
Bake in a water bath at 160°C for 90 minutes.
Leave in the oven with the door closed for 2 hours after turning it off (this prevents cracks).
Chill for 24 hours for the perfect dense, creamy texture.

name: Brownie (Sea Salt Fudgy)
steps: Melt chocolate and butter. Whisk sugar and eggs for 5 minutes until very frothy (creates the crackly top).
Fold the chocolate into the eggs.
Gently stir in flour and cocoa powder.
Bake at 175°C for 25 minutes. The center should still be slightly moist.
Sprinkle flaky sea salt over the top while hot. Let cool completely before cutting into squares.

name: Cookies (White Chocolate Macadamia)
steps: Brown the butter in a pan until it smells nutty and has brown specks; let cool.
Cream the browned butter with sugar and egg.
Fold in the nuts and white chocolate.
Chill the dough balls in the fridge for 24 hours (essential for flavor depth and texture).
Bake at 180°C for 10-12 minutes until the edges are just barely golden.

name: Gourmet Finale (French Macarons)
steps: Sift almond flour and powdered sugar three times to ensure zero lumps.
Whisk egg whites to stiff peaks, adding granulated sugar slowly.
MACARONAGE: Fold the dry mix into the whites until the batter flows like 'slow lava'.
Pipe 3cm circles and TAP THE TRAY hard on the counter to release air bubbles.
Let sit for 45 minutes until a skin forms (critical for the 'feet').
Bake at 150°C for 15 minutes. Sandwich with ganache once cooled.
